title: BLOB queries make crash Virtuoso
description:
Hello,
We are using the Virtuoso dev version from the commit 60ed0fe. We are also running Virtuoso on a Debian 9 machine.
Multiple time a day our instance of Virtuoso crashs with the following error:
03:02:28 bin/virtuoso-t(+0x533393) [0x562e4e411393]

03:02:28 /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6(clone+0x5f) [0x7f2fe08c59bf]
03:02:28 GPF: Dkpool.c:1945 large free not on 4k boundary

We have activated the log of the queries and every time our Virtuoso crashs. When we monitor these logs with the command:
SQL> SELECT ql_start_dt,ql_user,ql_sqlstate,ql_error,ql_text FROM sys_query_log WHERE qrl_file = 'queries_virtuoso.qrl';

The last query that Virtuoso got before crashing is always the same query, no exceptions:
2017.4.28 3:2.21 261027000  dba                                                                               NULL                                                                              NULL                                                                              BLOB 1049 chars

What means this BLOB 1049 chars query? Any idea of what to do to avoid this very annoying error?
Thanks in advance for any light on this.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_blob (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, data BLOB NOT NULL);
INSERT INTO test_blob (id, data) VALUES (1, 'Sample BLOB data');
INSERT INTO test_blob (id, data) VALUES (2, SUBSTR(REPLACE('A', 'A', 'A'), 1, 1049));
DELETE FROM test_blob WHERE id IS NULL;
key_bug_features: BLOB queries cause crashes in Virtuoso database
